title: Contrasting Visions in American Politics
source: The Guardian - The Nation – July - August 2026
content:
In recent years, American politics has been defined by deeply contrasting visions of the nation's past and future. Hillary Clinton's political approach was often criticized as being backward-looking, relying on a sense of steady continuity that ignored growing public frustration. Donald Trump's vision was equally backward-looking but manifested in a much more snarling and aggressive way. Trump was animated by right-wing grievances, viewing America as possessing a past greatness that had been stolen by a corrupt elite and conniving foreign regimes. Ultimately, it was this bleak vision that captured the anti-system anger that establishment politicians had foolishly ignored.

Despite his electoral success, Trump has not managed to build a durable political coalition. He remains a deeply polarizing figure who often struggles with historical unpopularity. In contrast to both Clinton and Trump, Senator Bernie Sanders has offered a politics that isn't focused on recovering a romanticized past. Instead, Sanders focuses on building a bridge between the fundamental promise of America and the actual fulfillment of that promise. Many political analysts now compare him to Barry Goldwater, noting that even if he never won the presidency, his progressive platform decisively shifted his party's future trajectory.
